course.title: Writing Your Life: The Personal Essay
course.cost: Free with library card, registration required, limited to 14

courseRecord.about: For adults who have a life worth writing about, which the instructor wishes to note is all of them. No writing experience required. Each week pairs a short craft discussion with writing time and gentle, structured feedback. The aim is one finished personal essay by the end of the session.

week.assignment: A list of ten true things you could write about and one you think you cannot.

week.topic: Beginnings. Finding the doorway into the material.
week.assignment: Three opening paragraphs for three different essays.

week.topic: Scene and detail. The concrete noun is your friend.
The hard subjects. Writing about loss and the people we love without sentimentality and without armor.
Workshop night. Half the class reads drafts aloud for structured response.
Workshop night, remaining readers, and endings. What an essay owes the reader at the door.
week.assignment: Final drafts due by email within one week for written comments.

groundRules.rule: What is read in this room stays in this room.
Feedback names what is working before what is not, and is always about the writing, never the life.
You may pass on reading aloud at any time, once.

courseRecord.personalNote: Left me privately thrilled and privately mortified. Signed up for whatever they offer in the spring.
